_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Cleft Lip and Cleft Palate Surgery (Per Stage)
specialty: Pediatric Surgery/ Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery/ Plastic & Reconstructive Surgery

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Has the patient presented with congenital cleft lip and cleft palate which may be complete or incomplete or submucous / with or without cleft lip?
Is there evidence of cleft palate on clinical photograph?

common_queries: Clinical notes (detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure) missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Clinical photograph at pre-authorization is missing or inadequate to show cleft lip/palate.
Claim submission lacks detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and line of treatment.
Pre-anesthesia check-up report is missing at claim submission.
Detailed procedure/Operative Notes are missing or do not adequately describe surgical steps at claim submission.
Post-procedure clinical photograph is missing at claim submission or does not demonstrate the post-treatment outcome.
Documentation does not clearly state if the patient has congenital cleft lip and cleft palate (complete/incomplete/submucous / with or without cleft lip).
Clinical photograph does not provide sufficient evidence of cleft palate.

clinical_key_pointers: Difficulty in feeding: new born child is not able to breast feed properly due to child's inability to suck efficiently. Nipples for bottle feeding present the same problem.
Difficulty in swallowing, as liquids or foods come out the nose.
Nasal speaking voice and various speech problems.
May have malformed and missing teeth.
